name: ab-test-analyzer
description: A/B test metric design and result analysis plugin. Designs experiment metrics from planning documents and performs statistical analysis on experiment data from Mixpanel or Google Sheets.

commands.name: ab-design
commands.description: Design A/B test metrics from a Notion planning document. Proposes primary/sub/guardrail metrics and outputs a metric spec matching Qanda's Metric Review template.

commands.name: ab-analyze
commands.description: Analyze A/B test results from Mixpanel or Google Sheets. Runs statistical significance tests and provides scenario-based decision recommendations.

agents.name: stat-analysis
agents.description: Statistical computation agent for A/B testing. Selects tests, executes Python-based significance testing, and returns structured numerical results.

skills.name: ab-test-metric-design
skills.description: This skill should be used when the user asks "어떤 지표 봐야 해?", "what metrics should I track", "성공 기준 정해줘", "PRD 보고 지표 설계해줘", "primary metric 뭘로 잡아야 해?", "guardrail metric이 뭐야?", or "가격 테스트 성공 기준". Provides metric selection frameworks with Qanda-specific patterns including the Primary/Sub/Guardrail hierarchy, experiment-type templates, and segment analysis requirements.

skills.name: ab-test-statistical-methods
skills.description: This skill should be used when the user asks "이 결과 유의미해?", "is this result statistically significant", "p-value 해석해줘", "신뢰구간 계산해줘", "효과크기 어때?", "카이제곱 써야 해 t-test 써야 해?", "얼마나 더 돌려야 유의미해져?", or "결과가 애매한데 어떻게 해?". Provides test selection logic, p-value interpretation, confidence interval computation, and a decision framework for inconclusive results with enforcement against p-hacking and optional stopping.
